metaTitle: Living with Back Pain? Bracing & Recovery Solutions | Avana Surgical
metaDescription: Doctor-recommended lumbar and cervical bracing for back pain, scoliosis, and spine recovery — fitted in India, dispatched by Osteokart.

hero.title: Stand taller. Move with less pain.
hero.description: From persistent lower back ache to disc issues and scoliosis — bracing combined with the right movement plan can change how your back feels every day.

conditionsHeading.title: What's behind your back pain?
conditionsHeading.lead: Back pain has many causes — and the right solution depends on which one is yours.

conditions.title: Mechanical low back pain
conditions.description: Pain from posture, lifting, or muscle strain. Lumbar bracing offloads the spine and supports recovery.

conditions.title: Disc-related pain
conditions.description: Pain or numbness from a herniated or bulging disc. Bracing limits the movements that aggravate symptoms while you heal.

conditions.title: Scoliosis
conditions.description: Spinal curvature, often first noticed in teens. Modern bracing has helped many patients reduce curve progression.

conditions.title: Postural ache
conditions.description: Forward head posture and rounded shoulders cause daily ache. A postural brace serves as a gentle reminder.

productsHeading.title: Solutions for back & spine pain
productsHeading.lead: Bracing matched to your specific spine condition — selected by Avana from world-leading brands and dispatched by Osteokart.

testimonials.name: Fawzul Hidhaya's Mother
testimonials.testimonial: My daughter was diagnosed with scoliosis at 11. After years of exercises, the curve reached 32°. Surgery was advised — but with Spinamic, before the brace it was 35°, and with the brace it reduced to 25°.

seoContent.title: Living with back pain — what to know
seoContent.intro: Back pain affects most adults at some point. Most cases resolve with conservative care, but knowing when bracing helps — and when to escalate — makes a real difference.

sections.heading: Mechanical vs structural
sections.body: Most back pain is mechanical — from strain, posture, or fatigue — and responds well to bracing combined with movement. Structural pain from disc, scoliosis, or fractures needs specialist input.

sections.heading: Why bracing helps
sections.body: A lumbar brace offloads stressed structures, limits painful motion, and lets healing happen. For scoliosis specifically, bracing during growth has strong clinical evidence for slowing curve progression.

sections.heading: Red flags — see a doctor
sections.body: Severe pain, leg numbness or weakness, changes in bladder or bowel control — these are warning signs. Bracing complements specialist care; it doesn't replace it.

seoContent.disclaimer: Educational content. Please consult a spine specialist for diagnosis and treatment.

faq.question: Will wearing a lumbar brace weaken my back muscles?
faq.answer: This is a common worry, but research shows it is largely a myth when bracing is paired with physiotherapy. Bracing supports recovery; rehabilitation strengthens the back.

faq.question: Can I sit at work with a lumbar brace?
faq.answer: Yes — most lumbar bracing is designed for daily use including seated work. Your specialist will recommend wear duration based on your condition.

faq.question: How long does scoliosis bracing take?
faq.answer: Most studies show meaningful curve stabilisation over 12–24 months of consistent wear during the growth period. Results depend on age, curve type, and compliance.

cta.title: Spine pain is personal — let us help you choose right.
